cadence_note: The fastest-moving accepted table is climate-daily, whose MSC Datamart mirror (https://dd.weather.gc.ca/today/climate/observations/) is rebuilt every day; climate-monthly publishes within weeks of month end. The rest move far slower: hydrometric statistics lag ~1y behind the water year, AHCCD is republished in multi-year batches (currently ending 2020), and climate-normals is a frozen 1981-2010 reference period. A weekly tick keeps worst-case staleness at a week for the daily table while re-pulling the full corpus (~2h, dominated by the ~211k Datamart station-year CSVs). ECCC publishes no release calendar for these collections, so the cadence is set by the mirror's daily rebuild, not by a stated schedule.
